message:
feat: --min-confidence threshold + --sarif export

Two output filters / formats that compose with everything (CLI, lib,
pre-commit hook, GitHub Action).

minConfidence:
- New CreateOptions.minConfidence + RedactOptions.minConfidence
  (per-call wins). Filter applied at the end of runDetection so
  every call site (detect, redact, classifyColumns, redactTable)
  inherits it.
- Rule spans always confidence:1.0 (deterministic) so they pass
  any threshold; this only filters model spans.
- Exposed as --min-confidence <0..1> in CLI subcommands and
  TEXTSIFT_PRECOMMIT_MIN_CONFIDENCE env in the precommit hook.
- Action input min-confidence forwards to the same env.

SARIF v2.1.0:
- New packages/textsift/src/sarif.ts module — pure data transform
  from DetectedSpan[] to SARIF log. Spec-compliant output:
  $schema, version, tool.driver.{name,version,informationUri,rules},
  results[].{ruleId, level, message, locations, partialFingerprints}.
- Per-label rule definitions for the 8 model categories with
  human-readable shortDescription. Custom-rule labels (from the
  secrets preset etc.) get synthesised metadata.
- Severity mapping: model spans → error (overridable via
  modelSpanLevel), rule:block → error, rule:warn → warning.
- Stable partialFingerprints (sha256 of file|line|label|text) so
  GitHub Code Scanning de-duplicates findings across runs.
- Exposed three ways:
  * CLI: `textsift detect file.txt --sarif > findings.sarif`
  * Pre-commit: `--sarif <path>` writes alongside human output
  * Action: `sarif-output:` input passes through to precommit.js
- Public subpath export at "textsift/sarif" — third-party tooling
  can convert their own DetectResult to SARIF without re-running
  the model.

Also:
- Added GitHub Code Scanning workflow example to the action docs
  page (warn-only action + codeql-action/upload-sarif → Security
  tab integration).

Tests:
- 13 SARIF unit tests (line/col across newlines, severity mapping,
  modelSpanLevel override, fingerprint stability, rule
  deduplication, schema URL, custom-rule synthesis).
- 5 new CLI integration tests (--min-confidence validation, range
  behavior, --sarif valid v2.1.0 output, file-path uri).
- 3 new precommit tests (--sarif file write, MIN_CONFIDENCE env
  validation, threshold semantics).

files.patch:
@@ -47,6 +47,20 @@ inputs:
       Pair with `actions/cache` to persist the 770 MB model across runs.
     required: false
     default: ''
+  min-confidence:
+    description: |
+      Drop spans below this confidence threshold (0..1). Default: `0`
+      — keep every span. Higher values trade recall for precision.
+    required: false
+    default: '0'
+  sarif-output:
+    description: |
+      If set to a file path, write SARIF v2.1.0 to that path. Pair
+      with `github/codeql-action/upload-sarif` to surface findings
+      in the repo's Security tab as proper alerts (not just PR
+      annotations).
+    required: false
+    default: ''
 
 runs:
   using: 'composite'
@@ -103,6 +117,7 @@ runs:
         TEXTSIFT_CACHE_DIR: ${{ inputs.cache-dir }}
         TEXTSIFT_PRECOMMIT_SEVERITY: ${{ inputs.severity }}
         TEXTSIFT_PRECOMMIT_SECRETS: ${{ inputs.secrets == 'true' && '1' || '0' }}
+        TEXTSIFT_PRECOMMIT_MIN_CONFIDENCE: ${{ inputs.min-confidence }}
       run: |
         if [ "${{ steps.files.outputs.count }}" = "0" ]; then
           echo "No files to scan."
@@ -122,4 +137,7 @@ runs:
         # workflow commands itself when GITHUB_ACTIONS=true.
         EXTRA=()
         if [ "${{ inputs.warn-only }}" = "true" ]; then EXTRA+=("--warn-only"); fi
+        if [ -n "${{ inputs.sarif-output }}" ]; then
+          EXTRA+=("--sarif" "${{ inputs.sarif-output }}")
+        fi
         xargs -a /tmp/textsift-files.txt node "$SCRIPT" "${EXTRA[@]}"

@@ -36,6 +36,8 @@ That's the minimum. The action handles cache, model download, file selection (PR
 | `secrets` | `'true'` | Set to `'false'` if you already use gitleaks / trufflehog. |
 | `textsift-version` | `'latest'` | npm version. Pin to a tag (`'0.1.0'`) for reproducible CI. |
 | `cache-dir` | runner default | Override the model cache location. |
+| `min-confidence` | `'0'` | Drop spans below this confidence (0..1). Trades recall for precision. |
+| `sarif-output` | `''` | If set, write SARIF v2.1.0 to that path. Pair with `codeql-action/upload-sarif` for the GitHub Security tab. |
 
 ## Common patterns
 
@@ -79,6 +81,32 @@ Glob is passed to `git ls-files`; respects `.gitignore`.
 
 textsift's regex secrets preset overlaps with gitleaks. If gitleaks is your secrets baseline, let textsift focus on what only the model catches (names, emails, phones, addresses, etc.).
 
+### GitHub Code Scanning integration (Security tab)
+
+```yaml
+permissions:
+  contents: read
+  security-events: write   # required to upload SARIF
+
+jobs:
+  pii:
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+        with: { fetch-depth: 0 }
+      - uses: teamchong/textsift@v1
+        with:
+          sarif-output: textsift.sarif
+          warn-only: 'true'   # let SARIF be the gating signal, not the action
+      - uses: github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@v3
+        if: always()
+        with:
+          sarif_file: textsift.sarif
+          category: textsift
+```
+
+After the first run, findings appear in the repo's **Security → Code scanning** tab as proper alerts (with assignment, dismissal, and history). PR check status comes from Code Scanning's rules, so you can configure `warn-only: 'true'` on the action and let GitHub gate the merge based on the alert severity.
+
 ### Pin the version for reproducible CI
 
 ```yaml

@@ -9,6 +9,7 @@
   "exports": {
     ".": "./dist/index.js",
     "./browser": "./dist/browser/index.js",
+    "./sarif": "./dist/sarif.js",
     "./package.json": "./package.json"
   },
   "bin": {
@@ -41,7 +42,7 @@
   "scripts": {
     "build": "npm run build:browser && npm run build:native",
     "build:browser": "mkdir -p dist/browser && npm run build:zig && npm run build:opt && node scripts/inline-wasm.mjs && bun build src/browser/index.ts --outdir dist/browser --target browser --format esm --sourcemap=external && bun x tsc --emitDeclarationOnly",
-    "build:native": "bash scripts/build-native.sh && bun build src/index.ts --outdir dist --target node --format esm --sourcemap=external && bun build src/cli.ts --outdir dist --target node --format esm --sourcemap=external",
+    "build:native": "bash scripts/build-native.sh && bun build src/index.ts --outdir dist --target node --format esm --sourcemap=external && bun build src/cli.ts --outdir dist --target node --format esm --sourcemap=external && bun build src/sarif.ts --outdir dist --target node --format esm --sourcemap=external",
     "build:zig": "npm run build:zig-st && npm run build:zig-mt",
     "build:zig-st": "mise exec -- zig build-exe -target wasm32-freestanding -mcpu=generic+simd128+relaxed_simd -O ReleaseFast -fstrip -flto src/zig/wasm_exports.zig -cflags -O3 -mrelaxed-simd -flto -- src/c/fma.c --name textsift -fno-entry -rdynamic && mv textsift.wasm dist/browser/",
     "build:zig-mt": "mise exec -- zig build-exe -target wasm32-freestanding -mcpu=generic+simd128+relaxed_simd+atomics+bulk_memory -O ReleaseFast -fstrip -flto src/zig/wasm_exports.zig -cflags -O3 -mrelaxed-simd -flto -- src/c/fma.c --shared-memory --import-memory --initial-memory=4194304 --max-memory=2147483648 --export=__stack_pointer --name textsift-mt -fno-entry -rdynamic && mv textsift-mt.wasm dist/browser/",
